footer.brand.desc: MCP tools for Unreal Editor UMG, Blueprint, Animation, and Material workflows.
footer.bottom.copy: © 2026 UMG MCP. The public core is MIT licensed.
hero.badge: Fab v1.0.13 · Open source v1.0.12 · UE 5.6 / 5.7 / 5.8 · Win64
hero.subtitle: 89 MCP tools for Unreal Editor across widgets, Blueprints, animation, materials, and asset workflows. Both editions share the core; Fab adds an in-editor AI workflow layer.
baseline.title: Documentation baseline
baseline.text: The open-source MCP baseline is v1.0.12 (internal version 8), audited against public commit adab2ea on 2026-07-23; the Fab release line is v1.0.13 (internal version 9).
order.label: Recommended reading order
order.title: One path, read from top to bottom
order.subtitle: Understand the product boundary, choose an edition, learn the tools, connect Unreal, then enter the editing loop.
order.1.title: What it is
order.1.desc: Separate the core, MCP bridge, and Fab layer
order.2.title: Choose an edition
order.2.desc: The real Fab vs open-source difference
order.3.title: Learn the tools
order.4.title: Understand connection
order.4.desc: External MCP vs in-editor native path
order.5.title: Run a task
order.6.title: Start using it
product.label: Step 1 · Understand the product
product.title: UMG MCP is a three-layer system, not a single chat box
product.subtitle: Once the layers are separated, the relationship between Fab, open source, Python, and C++ is straightforward.
product.layer1.title: Unreal C++ command core
product.layer1.desc: Reads and changes UMG, Blueprint, Sequencer, Material, and editor assets on the Game Thread. Core improvements are synchronized to the public repository.
product.layer2.title: External MCP interface
product.layer2.desc: Resources/Python/UmgMcpServer.py exposes 89 tools to MCP clients such as Codex, Claude, and Gemini and handles multi-editor discovery and connection.
product.layer3.title: In-editor AI workflow layer
product.layer3.desc: Native Slate chat, model provider settings, Agent/Task orchestration, history, context compression, Tool Modes, approvals, and local LiteRT-LM inference.
product.rule.title: The most important counting rule
product.rule.desc: The 89 tools are the public external MCP Server surface. Fab's built-in chat exposes a curated native tool set according to Tool Mode and also uses orchestration pseudo-tools such as task_begin and task_end. It is not a second set of 89 tools.
editions.label: Step 2 · Choose an edition
editions.title: Open source and Fab: the same core, different workflow experience
editions.subtitle: The old claim that the open-source APIs are weaker was inaccurate. The real differences are installation, in-editor AI, and the production workflow layer.
editions.row.sessions: Dynamic ports, multi-editor discovery, exclusive sessions
editions.row.install.oss: Clone source and compile with UBT
editions.row.install.fab: Install the matching package through Fab / Epic Launcher
editions.row.external.fab: Also requires uv/Python when using the external route
editions.row.license.fab: Fab plugin package under Fab license terms
editions.row.direct: Built-in chat calls C++ tools directly, without Python
editions.row.providers.oss: Handled by the external MCP client
editions.row.providers.fab: Google login/API, Zhipu, custom OpenAI-compatible, Codex CLI, LiteRT-LM
editions.row.history: Session history and optional context compression
editions.pick.oss.title: You need protocol review, source changes, or an existing AI client
editions.pick.oss.desc: Best for developers, researchers, and teams comfortable maintaining the Python/uv MCP environment.
editions.pick.fab.title: You want Launcher installation and an AI workflow directly inside UE
editions.pick.fab.desc: Best for artists, technical artists, and production teams that want built-in providers, agents, history, and local models.
tools.label: Step 3 · Learn the tools
tools.title: 89 MCP tools in 8 workflow-oriented groups
tools.subtitle: Counted from the current UmgMcpServer.py: 86 domain tools plus 3 connection tools.
tools.cta: Inspect all 89 tools one by one →
architecture.label: Step 4 · Understand connection
architecture.title: Two entry points, one C++ command core
architecture.subtitle: Fab needs no Python only for its built-in chat route. Fab can also use the external MCP route, and that route still uses the Python stdio server.
architecture.external.title: External MCP route
architecture.external.a: All 89 public tools belong to this MCP surface.
architecture.external.b: Discovery records and dynamic ports distinguish multiple UE instances.
architecture.external.c: Writes are serialized onto the Game Thread to avoid concurrent asset mutation.
architecture.native.title: Native in-editor route
architecture.native.a: No Python server is started; tool calls are dispatched inside the plugin.
architecture.native.b: Tool Modes filter tools per task and do not expose all 89 tools by default.
architecture.native.c: Tasks, history, approvals, context compression, and local models live in this layer.
workflow.label: Step 5 · Run a task
workflow.title: Every reliable edit follows the same closed loop
workflow.subtitle: Do not jump straight from a UI request to writing. Connect, scope a target, read real state, make the smallest change, and verify.
workflow.1.title: Start and connect
workflow.1.desc: Open UE. External clients call list_umg_mcp_servers and then connect_umg_mcp. The Fab native route opens UMG AI Assistant directly.
workflow.2.title: Choose the target
workflow.2.desc: Call set_target_umg_asset or set_target_blueprint_asset, then focus a widget, function, or animation when needed.
workflow.3.title: Read real state
workflow.3.desc: Read the widget tree, properties, layout, BlueCode roundtrip data, animation overview, or material graph instead of guessing from a screenshot.
workflow.4.title: Plan the smallest change
workflow.4.desc: Prefer union writes. When changing an existing BlueCode graph, carry base_revision/source_map and use dry_run first when useful.
workflow.5.title: Handle deletion explicitly
workflow.5.desc: Use the matching delete tool with confirm_delete=true for widgets, nodes, variables, animations, tracks, keys, or HLSL items.
workflow.6.title: Compile and save
workflow.6.desc: Use bluecode_compile/compile_blueprint for Blueprint, hlsl_compile for HLSL, and save_asset for other assets.
workflow.7.title: Read back and verify
workflow.7.desc: Read the changed scope again, check structure, properties, links, keys, and compiler diagnostics, then finish the task.
start.label: Step 6 · Start using it
start.title: Install your chosen edition and complete the first connection
start.subtitle: The full commands, configuration, and verification steps are kept on one setup page.
start.oss.title: GitHub source + external MCP client
start.oss.1: Clone into Plugins/UmgMcp in your project.
start.oss.2: Compile and enable the plugin with UE 5.8 on Win64.
start.oss.4: Discover a UE instance, connect, and set a target.
start.fab.title: Launcher installation + in-editor AI
start.fab.1: Install the package matching UE 5.6, 5.7, or 5.8.
start.fab.2: Enable UmgMcp and restart the editor.
start.fab.3: Configure Google, Zhipu, a custom API, Codex CLI, or LiteRT-LM.
start.fab.4: Open UMG AI Assistant and choose a Tool Mode.
status.label: Version status and known boundaries
status.title: The documentation states both what works now and what is not yet guaranteed
status.release.title: Release line
status.release.desc: Fab is v1.0.13 / internal version 9; the open-source MCP core is v1.0.12 / internal version 8. Fab builds cover UE 5.6, 5.7, and 5.8 on Win64, and this submission uses fresh artifact URLs so Fab performs a new scan.
status.bluecode.title: BlueCode remains transitional
status.bluecode.desc: Union, dry-run, revisions, source maps, right anchors, and rollback are implemented. Arbitrary nested CFGs, loops, Sequence/Switch, latent nodes, and irreducible graphs remain future work.
status.surface.title: Tool surfaces are different concepts
status.surface.desc: The 89 public tools, Fab Agent Tool Modes, and raw C++ CommandTypes are three layers. This documentation does not combine their counts.
status.safety.title: Safe writes
status.safety.desc: Incremental writes preserve omitted content by default. Destructive operations use dedicated delete tools and explicit confirmation. Read-back and compile verification are still required.
reference.label: Go deeper
reference.title: Move from the overview to exact protocols
reference.subtitle: This page provides linear understanding. The catalog and protocol documents provide exact names, payloads, and boundaries.
reference.tools.title: Catalog of all 89 MCP tools
reference.tools.desc: Purpose, mutation type, and compatibility status for every tool in 8 groups.
reference.start.title: Installation and first connection
reference.start.desc: Complete open-source and Fab setup routes.
reference.widget.desc: Targets, reads, union writes, and deletion contracts.
reference.bluecode.desc: Semantic reads, roundtrip data, revisions, dry-run, and rollback.
reference.animation.desc: Animation scopes, time slices, tracks, keys, and deletion.
reference.material.desc: Material graphs, HLSL, parameters, outputs, and compilation.
catalog.badge: Main path step 3 · Complete tool catalog
catalog.title: All 89 MCP tools, from connection through compilation
catalog.subtitle: Audited directly against open-source v1.0.12 Resources/Python/UmgMcpServer.py: 3 connection tools plus 86 domain tools.
catalog.scope.title: What does this number include?
catalog.scope.desc: It includes the 89 tools actually registered by the external MCP Server. Fab orchestration pseudo-tools such as task_begin/task_end and raw C++ CommandTypes that are not wrapped by MCP are not mixed into the count.
catalog.search.label: Search by tool name or purpose
catalog.search.placeholder: For example: bluecode, delete, keyframe, material
catalog.native.label: Fab native tool-surface note
catalog.native.title: Fab Agents use a filtered tool surface, not another 89-item list
catalog.native.subtitle: This was the most confusing part of the old documentation.
catalog.native.mode.title: Tool Modes
catalog.native.mode.desc: UMG, Blueprint, Animation, and Material modes expose task-specific allowlists to the built-in Agent, reducing accidental calls and context noise.
catalog.native.task.desc: These pseudo-tools drive Fab Agent/Task handoff and UI presentation. They are not part of the external MCP Server's 89 tools.
catalog.native.raw.title: Raw C++ CommandTypes
catalog.native.raw.desc: The dispatcher may support additional helper commands. A command counts as a public MCP tool only after UmgMcpServer.py registers it.
catalog.next.title: Next: install and complete the first read-only loop
catalog.next.desc: Connect Unreal, set a target, and read the widget tree. Only write after confirming that the result is correct.
setup.title: Choose one route and follow it from 1 to done
setup.subtitle: Open source is source code plus external MCP. Fab additionally provides Launcher installation plus in-editor AI. Do not mix the dependencies of the two routes.
setup.rule.title: First decide where the conversation runs
setup.rule.desc: External MCP clients such as Codex, Claude, and Gemini use the Python/uv stdio server with either edition. Chatting directly inside Unreal Editor is available only in Fab and does not require Python.
setup.oss.title: GitHub source + external MCP client
setup.oss.subtitle: The current public source baseline is UE 5.8 / Win64. You need Git, a working C++ toolchain, uv, and an AI client with MCP stdio support.
setup.oss.1.title: Clone the repository into Plugins/UmgMcp
setup.oss.1.note: Keep the final directory name UmgMcp for consistent configuration and troubleshooting.
setup.oss.2.title: Open the project in UE 5.8 and compile the plugin
setup.oss.2.desc: Enable UmgMcp under Plugins, let Unreal Build Tool build the Editor module, and restart. When upgrading from an older build, remove stale Binaries/Intermediate for this plugin first.
setup.oss.3.title: Install uv and confirm the Python server can start
setup.oss.3.note: The MCP client normally starts the server. Run this command directly only for troubleshooting.
setup.oss.4.title: Add UMG MCP to the client configuration
setup.oss.4.note: The directory must point to Resources/Python. Escape Windows backslashes in JSON.
setup.oss.5.title: Complete the first read-only connection
setup.oss.5.note: Confirm the correct project, asset, and widget tree before any write operation.
setup.oss.6.title: Optional: run static protocol checks
setup.fab.title: Fab / Epic Launcher + in-editor AI
setup.fab.subtitle: v1.0.13 packages cover UE 5.6, 5.7, and 5.8 on Win64. All three use fresh download URLs and pass the forbidden-executable scan.
setup.fab.1.title: Get the plugin on Fab and install the matching engine package
setup.fab.1.desc: Do not manually copy a 5.8 package into 5.6. Select the plugin build matching your UE version in the Launcher.
setup.fab.2.title: Enable UmgMcp and restart the editor
setup.fab.2.desc: The package includes UmgMcp, ChatWithUnreal, and LiteRTLMUnreal modules. Fully restart UE after first enablement or a version change.
setup.fab.3.title: Configure a provider under Editor Preferences → Plugins → UmgMcp
setup.fab.3.note: Configure only the routes you plan to use. API keys, login, local CLI, and local models can be combined with priority and retry policies.
setup.fab.4.title: Open UMG AI Assistant and choose a Tool Mode
setup.fab.4.desc: UMG, Blueprint, Animation, and Material modes provide different allowlists to the built-in Agent. Complex work can also be decomposed by the Task/Agent layer.
setup.fab.5.title: Start with one explicit, verifiable task
setup.fab.prompt: Open /Game/UI/WBP_LoginPanel. Read the widget tree and root layout first. Do not modify anything. Tell me the current structure and which tools you plan to use.
setup.fab.5.note: After confirming the read is correct, send a second message requesting the incremental edit.
setup.fab.6.title: If you still want external Codex or Claude to control the Fab package
setup.fab.6.desc: You can. Use the Resources/Python MCP configuration from Route A. uv/Python is still required because you selected the external MCP route.
setup.trouble.label: Troubleshooting
setup.trouble.title: Verify the connection layer before individual tools
setup.trouble.connection: Confirm UE is open and the plugin is enabled. Run list_umg_mcp_servers again and connect to a live dynamic port rather than an old fixed port.
setup.trouble.multi: List every instance, choose by project/path, connect with exclusive=true, and set the target.
setup.trouble.target.symptom: A tool succeeded but changed the wrong asset
setup.trouble.target: Call get_target_umg_asset or get_target_blueprint_asset first and pass the full asset path before writing.
setup.trouble.compile.symptom: Blueprint or HLSL changes appear inactive
setup.trouble.compile: Run the matching compile tool, inspect diagnostics, then read the graph or material again.
setup.trouble.fab.symptom: A UE version is not visible on Fab
setup.trouble.fab: Check the listing update review state. The v1.0.13 package line includes 5.6/5.7/5.8 and uses a new version number and download URLs distinct from the rejected submission.
setup.next.title: After connecting, return to the standard editing loop
setup.next.desc: Target → Read → Plan → Write → Compile/Save → Read Back.
